fields.definition_from_readme: Unique project ID number. Reformatted for this release.  Users can convert from the old format to the new by appending L1000003 to the right five digits from the old IDs.

fields.definition_from_readme: Name of the project as it is reported by sources, appended to fuel type and country/area information.
A determination of whether the project is a gas, oil, or oil and gas project.
The country/area that the project is in. If a project spans country borders, both countries are listed in the project name.
The subnational state that the project is in (province, state, etc.).
Tag stating if production from a field uses conventional or unconventional methods.
The status of development of a project, e.g., discovered, in development, operating, mothballed, decommissioned, abandoned, or UGS.

value_definitions.name: Discovered
value_definitions.definition: A unit is typically considered to be discovered when there has been a well drilled into a particular formation, and it yielded some significant quantity of oil and/or gas. Discovered units are not necessarily economic to exploit.

value_definitions.name: In-development
value_definitions.definition: Following discovery, the process of moving toward commercial production has begun. This means that a company is planning to develop the project, as evidenced by one or more of the following criteria being reached: the company has applied for approval for commercial production (if needed in the jurisdiction), the project has reached the Final Investment Decision (FID), a final environmental impact statement has been published, and/or the drilling of development (not appraisal) wells and/or adding takeaway capacity (infrastructure such as pipelines, storage tanks, etc.) to enable commercial production has begun.

value_definitions.name: Operating
value_definitions.definition: The unit has commercial production of oil/gas (that is, quantities that are being sold to market).

value_definitions.name: Abandoned
value_definitions.definition: Operations have ceased, but the wells/unit were not decommissioned following all the usual steps, such as plugging the wells (including filling the well with cement).

value_definitions.name: Mothballed
value_definitions.definition: Operations temporarily shut down; may be restarted.

value_definitions.name: Decommissioning
value_definitions.definition: Operations permanently ceased; infrastructure is typically removed.

value_definitions.name: UGS
value_definitions.definition: Exhausted units may be converted from producing to serving as underground gas storage (UGS) facilities; these are labeled as “UGS.”

value_definitions.name: Exploration
value_definitions.definition: An unproven concessionary block or region that potentially contains an undiscovered field is actively being explored for reserves.

value_definitions.name: Cancelled
value_definitions.definition: A discovered or in-development field that did not reach commercial production. This is evidenced by a formal acknowledgment by a company involved that a project is not going forward.

value_definitions.name: Underground gas storage
value_definitions.definition: Exhausted fields may be converted from producing to serving as underground gas storage (UGS) facilities; these are labeled as “UGS” or “UGS (converted).”

fields.definition_from_readme: Additional information on the status, see methodology for details.
The year of the source of the status information.
Year in which the project was discovered. If the project is made up of multiple fields, the earliest discovery year is listed here.
Year in which the project reach final investment decision. If the project is made up of multiple fields, the earliest FID year is listed here.
Year in which the project started commercial production.If the project is made up of multiple fields, the earliest FID year is listed here.
The company that is the designated operator of the project.

fields.definition_from_readme: Alternate names for a project-level project.
Location of the project. Since each project spans some area (often many miles), the coordinates provided are approximately the middle of the project.
Location of the project. Since each project spans some area (often many miles), the coordinates provided are approximately the middle of the project.

Locations are designated as "exact" if there is information about where exactly the project is (e.g., coordinates explicitly stated, or project shown on a map). If only general information  is available (e.g., which state or county the project is in), an "approximate" designation is used.

If the location of the project is calculated from the location of the field-level projects which make it up, it is tagged as "location derived from fields". If data for the location is at the project level, this is tagged as "project level location".
Tag stating if a field predominantly located on or offshore.
Field-level projects outline in well-known-text format.
Each field-level project is within a particular basin (short for sedimentary basin), when available, shown here.
